Professional journey — roles, companies, growth, and impact.

## Career Path

Data Analyst
ICICI Bank Limited | May 2014 - July 2016
• Developed optimized SQL queries for data extraction and collaborated with stakeholders to gather data requirements.
• Delivered reports and dashboards using TIBCO Spotfire to support business analysis, campaign execution, and performance monitoring.

Data Engineer
Value Labs | Client: Nielsen - Media | December 2016 - September 2022
• Led reporting team delivering near real-time user and usage data insights using Big Data technologies (Hadoop, Spark, Hive).
• Migrated on-premises Big Data infrastructure to AWS (EMR, EC2, RDS, Athena, Lambda) for scalable data processing and analytics.
• Built Hive/Spark jobs within Oozie workflows, optimized SQL performance, and enabled ad-hoc querying through Athena and Lambda.

Senior Data Engineer
Value Labs | Client: eyos.one - Retail | October 2022 - September 2023
• Developed real-time and batch ETL pipelines to load data into MySQL and Snowflake, including Spark Streaming archival from S3 file sources.
• Built scalable, config-driven solutions to process sales data from various file formats and schemas.
• Created Python automation jobs for data aggregation and transformation; developed AWS Lambda functions for serverless task automation.

Senior Data Engineer
Value Labs | Client: Nielsen - Media | October 2023 - July 2024
• Designed and developed scalable data ingestion pipelines using PySpark and SQL within Dockerized Python images, integrated with Airflow for S3/Data Lake extraction.
• Built complex PySpark workflows for data transformation and cleaning supporting analytics and ML use cases, executed on AWS EMR and Kubernetes (EKS).
• Developed and maintained data warehouse solutions using HiveQL and Spark SQL with monitoring and alerting for job reliability and data quality.
• Collaborated with data scientists and analysts to deliver clean datasets; built dashboards in Superset and Spotfire for daily operational trend visualization.

Lead Engineer - Software Development (AI & Platform Engineering)
Quest Global | Client: Apple - Ad Platforms | August 2024 - Present
• Developed Ads Airflow MCP Server providing Claude AI direct read-only access to Ads Airflow environments, enabling AI-assisted infrastructure monitoring and debugging.
• Built AI-assisted code migration tool using RAG + multi-phase agentic pipeline (Claude Opus, Qdrant, LibCST) to automate Airflow 2.9.2 to 3.2.0 DAG migration across 100+ production DAGs, reducing manual effort by 80%.
• Developed an On-call (L1) Support AI Skill integrating multiple MCPs (PagerDuty, Slack, Splunk, Grafana) for automated alert debugging and resolution.
• Fine-tuned a RAG chatbot to handle consumer queries on Ads Airflow Slack support channel, reducing response time by 60% using curated documentation as knowledge base.
• Built LLM-maintained knowledge wiki using Claude Code with schema-driven extraction pipeline, generating browsable Wikipedia-style static HTML from raw sources (PDFs, markdown, HTML).
• Deployed and maintained end-to-end Apache Airflow data pipelines on Kubernetes (AWS EKS), achieving 99.99% uptime through optimized scheduling, Helm upgrades, executor tuning, and autoscaling.
• Containerized Airflow components with Docker and built CI/CD pipelines for reproducible deployments across dev/stg/prod environments.
• Implemented monitoring, alerting, and logging with Datadog/Grafana and Splunk; proactively troubleshot DAG failures, Kubernetes bottlenecks, and infrastructure incidents.
• Provided 24/7 production support including DAG deployment issues, failure investigations, and workflow debugging for onboarded teams.

The beginning of my story — where it all started.

## Where It Began

I born in a villege called Chennavaram, 2 km from the town called Porumamilla. My mother name Subhan Bee and father name is Imamsab, both used to work as daily labours, I have one elder sister Asin. I started my education in a Govt school at the age of 6 and studied in the same villege till 3rd standard. My parents moved to the town Yerraguntla in 1998 to give better education to me and my sister.

I enjoyed my childhood in a villege where there was no proper electrity and other facilities like roads, etc. I used to play a lot in my villege, enjoyed the villege life fully till I turned 9 years of age.

Name: Dastagiri Vadapalli
Date of Birth: October 1, 1989
Place: Chennavaram, Porumamilla
District: Kadapa (Rayalaseema)

What drives me forward — goals, dreams, and the road ahead.

## Looking Ahead

My short-term ambition is to land in to a AI Engineer role with good CTC by the end of the year 2026.
Get out of the EMI's by end of 2030 or have minimum of less than 40% of your salary.

Career aspiration is to do the M.tech from IIT Madras in AI/ML 
Start a company in home town to help the under privileged talented students to achieve their goals and land in their dream career


When I was a kid, I dreamed of becoming a Police Officer.
 My father often spoke about the bravery of officers — especially Umesh Chandra, IPS — and those stories left a deep impression on me. I imagined myself wearing that uniform one day.
But dreams alone aren’t enough. I had no idea how to become an IPS officer — no one to guide me, and I never asked my teachers either. Deep down, I also knew my parents couldn’t afford the expenses for such preparation.
So, like many others around me, I followed the crowd.
 My sister casually mentioned that most students were taking M.P.C. and doing Engineering. I did the same — without questioning whether it aligned with my dream.
🎓 Fast forward to my final year of Engineering — our college didn’t have campus placements, and reality hit hard. I suddenly remembered my childhood dream and started exploring police recruitment exams.
I applied for the Indian Air Force, cleared the written test with limited preparation, and got shortlisted for the SSB interview.
 That was my first-ever interview. I travelled to Mysore, stayed overnight at the railway station, and reported at 6 AM sharp — only to find hundreds of talented candidates from all over India.
I gave my best, but I wasn’t confident enough.
 I struggled during group discussions and was ultimately rejected. That was my first failure.
Soon after, the Andhra Pradesh Government released the Sub-Inspector (SI) recruitment notification in 2011, the same year I graduated. I saw it as a second chance.
I trained hard for the physical tests — 5 km run, 100 m sprint, 800 m run, long jump, high jump, and shot put. I completed the 5 km run in 20 minutes (against the 25-minute limit) and scored merit in events.
Now, all that stood between me and my dream was the final written test —

Paper 1: Arithmetic & Reasoning ✅ (my strong area)

Paper 2: General Studies ❌ (my weak area — History, Geography, Economy, Current Affairs)

For the first time, the exam introduced negative marking. I did well in Paper 1 but struggled in Paper 2. When the results came, I fell 10 marks short — only because of negative marking.
If not for that change, I might have been wearing the uniform I once dreamed of.
That was my second failure.
I had already spent two years after graduation preparing for this goal. The next notification was delayed due to the Telangana bifurcation, and I couldn’t afford to wait any longer. My parents needed financial support, so I took a different path — IT.
I later realized the real mistake wasn’t failure — it was not knowing the right path early enough.
 If I had spoken to a Police Officer or career counsellor, I would’ve known that to become an SI, it’s better to take H.E.C. in 12th and pursue a B.A. degree (History, Economics, Civics, Political Science, Sociology).
I learned it the hard way — too late to apply it.
Today, I’m happy with my job, my growth, and my career in IT. But every time I see a Police Officer, I can’t help but remember that boyhood dream and the mistakes that shaped my journey.
To every student or youngster reading this 👇
Don’t just follow the crowd.
Don’t choose a path because everyone else does.
Ask questions, seek guidance, and learn early what it takes to reach your goal.
Your dreams deserve clarity — not confusion.
And remember: failure doesn’t define you — lack of direction does.

Core beliefs, motivations, and what keeps me going.

## My Philosophy

I was born into a humble family in a small village where we didn’t even have electricity. My parents were daily wage earners, working tirelessly just to make ends meet.
I began my education in a government school, and later, my parents moved to a nearby town so my sister and I could get a better education, while they worked even harder to manage our expenses.
In 12th grade, I stayed in a hostel. After that, I decided to move to Hyderabad to pursue a B.Tech., dreaming of working part-time to support myself. But since my college was far from the city, that didn’t work out. So, I shared a small room with friends to minimise expenses and stayed focused on completing my degree.
I didn’t have much growing up — no bicycle, no video games, not even a cricket bat. But what I did have was love, resilience, and unforgettable childhood memories.
Fast forward to today —
 I live in one of the best cities in the world, work for an MNC, and have a home, car, and a beautiful family.
And here’s the truth:
 I was never a topper.
 I was never the most talented.
 But I was consistent, curious, and grateful for every opportunity that came my way.
My parents never pressured me to achieve something big — they simply gave me freedom and trust, and that made all the difference.
👉 You don’t need to be extraordinary to build a remarkable life.
 All you need is freedom, time, consistency, and the courage to take small steps every single day.

✨ Embracing Change for Personal Growth ✨

Reflecting on my journey, I used to immerse myself in motivational quotes and videos whenever I hit a low point. However, I realized that despite the inspiration, I wasn't implementing these lessons in my life. It's easy to get lost in the multitude of advice and struggle to commit to a single change.

Growing up, I unintentionally followed this pattern, never truly committing to any transformation. Recently, I had an awakening about this cycle and decided to break free from it. I made a conscious choice to stop merely reading quotes and, instead, vowed to implement every insight into my daily life.

That decision led me to a profound quote on LinkedIn that resonated deeply with me: 

" Without commitment, you will never start..
without consistency, you will never finish.. "

 It struck a chord, and I made a firm commitment to apply this principle in my life.

Today, I encourage you to reflect on your own journey. Are you simply consuming motivational content, or are you actively incorporating those lessons into your life? Let's make a collective effort to not just start, but to commit and stay consistent on our paths to personal growth. Remember, it's through commitment and consistency that we truly bring about lasting change. 💪✨

From Dreams to Decisions: The Story Behind My First Home, Car & iPhone 🏠🚗📱
When I started my first job, I was deeply fascinated by my colleagues’ iPhones, Royal Enfield bikes, and cars. As an automobile enthusiast since childhood, I often imagined owning one myself. With some savings finally appearing in my account, I began planning to buy a Used Car and an iPhone.
I had complete freedom — my parents never restricted my choices — yet something inside held me back. One day, it struck me: we were still living in a rented house and had never owned a home.
That thought changed everything.
My parents had invested their lifetime savings to buy a 1,100 sq. ft. plot, conduct my sister’s marriage, and support my education. I was able to complete my graduation with a fee reimbursement facility and had no plans for further studies.
The very next day, I decided to apply for my first-ever bank loan — a home loan — to build a house for my parents on that land they had bought years ago. The day construction started remains one of the most fulfilling moments of my life.
Years later, with steady career growth and salary hikes, I finally bought a New Car for my family and an iPhone for myself, achieving two long-awaited dreams — 5 years and 7 years in the making.
No one guided me through this journey. I simply followed my instincts and a bit of common sense — to prioritize what truly matters before pursuing desires.
It taught me one important life lesson:
Fulfillment doesn’t come from what we own, but from what we build for the people we love. ❤️

Recently, I have cleared the AWS AI Practitioner Certification.
I followed a simple process to prepare for this certification exam.
Finding the time for preparation and committing to that time regularly is difficult in a typical busy week, managing work-life balance.
I go to the office in a hybrid mode, 3 days a week, and it takes me two hours of travel time per day. I started spending those two hours of travel time when I’m working from home for the certification preparation. With this, I managed to spend 4 hours a week on weekdays without sacrificing anything in my routine and managed to spend 2-3 hours more on weekends.

I set this process and utilised this time mainly to learn new AI concepts that are necessary for certification, taking notes and refining them to post on LinkedIn so that others may benefit from it. I posted about 50+ posts on the concepts/topics that I learnt/prepared for the certification every week consistently, which helped me to revise the concepts whenever I needed and understand those concepts in another way.

This certification and setting up the process for preparation and maintaining that consistency is an example of commitment to something. All you need to do is set the process and believe in it.

I enjoyed this process rather than clearing the certification exam; you should believe in your process, whether you succeed or fail, the result doesn’t matter.

This certification gave me confidence in the process that I tried for the first time.
I can set and apply a similar process for other things, like upskilling, another certification, a workout, etc.. without worrying about the result, as I always believe in the process that I set.

Ordinary Student’s (My) Journey 🎓➡️💼
The school where I studied till the 10th doesn’t exist anymore.
 The intermediate college where I did my 12th — gone too.
 Even the engineering college where I completed my B. Tech was eventually shut down due to management issues.
But you know what still exists?
 👉 The certificates I earned.
 👉 The lessons I learned.
 👉 And the journey that shaped me.
I was an average student, the first graduate in my family, and had no one to guide me in my studies. I chose B.Tech simply because my friends did — no grand vision, no clear goal.
My real education, however, came from the experiences — studying in Rayalaseema (Kadapa) till 10th, Coastal Andhra (Nellore) for 12th, and Telangana (Hyderabad) for B. Tech. These journeys helped me meet people from diverse backgrounds and shaped my outlook on life.
I never imagined working for an MNC or living in a city like Hyderabad. I didn’t have big dreams or clear goals — just a desire to keep moving forward and make the best out of every situation.
Whatever I am today is the result of small, consistent decisions, learning from people around me, and never stopping, even when things were unclear.
If I could go back to my school days, I’d study with purpose, clarity, and passion. But life taught me something important —
🎯 It’s never too late to start dreaming big or working towards something meaningful.
🎯 It’s okay if you don’t have everything figured out right now.
🎯 What matters is that you keep learning, stay curious, and make the most of every opportunity that comes your way.
🎯 Your journey may start small — but your mindset decides how far you’ll go.
Keep believing, keep building — one step at a time. 💪

- Core values
- Sources of motivation
- Books/people that influenced you
- Life philosophy
- Advice you'd give your younger self

But you know what still exists?
👉 The certificates I earned.
👉 The lessons I learned.
👉 And the journey that shaped me.
I was an average student, the first graduate in my family, and had no one to guide me in my studies. I chose B.Tech simply because my friends did — no grand vision, no clear goal.
My real education, however, came from the experiences — studying in Rayalaseema (Kadapa) till 10th, Coastal Andhra (Nellore) for 12th, and Telangana (Hyderabad) for B. Tech. These journeys helped me meet people from diverse backgrounds and shaped my outlook on life.
I never imagined working for an MNC or living in a city like Hyderabad. I didn’t have big dreams or clear goals — just a desire to keep moving forward and make the best out of every situation.
Whatever I am today is the result of small, consistent decisions, learning from people around me, and never stopping, even when things were unclear.
If I could go back to my school days, I’d study with purpose, clarity, and passion. But life taught me something important —
🎯 It’s never too late to start dreaming big or working towards something meaningful.
🎯 It’s okay if you don’t have everything figured out right now.
🎯 What matters is that you keep learning, stay curious, and make the most of every opportunity that comes your way.
🎯 Your journey may start small — but your mindset decides how far you’ll go.
Keep believing, keep building — one step at a time. 💪

  • Sources of motivation
  • Books/people that influenced you
  • Life philosophy
  • Advice you’d give your younger self

Financial growth, investments, and lessons in building wealth.

## Financial Journey

My financial jpourney started with my First Job at ICICI Bank.

I have a own house in my home town Yerraguntla, construction completed in the year 2017.
I have a car, CUV, Ford Freestyle, Diesel, 1.5 cc purchased in the year 2019.
I have a purchased a new 2 BHK flat in Hyderabad in December, 2025.

10 Years, 10 Milestones – A Journey of Growth & Gratitude 🚀
Looking back at the last decade, I realize how much life has transformed – both personally and professionally. Here are some defining moments that shaped my journey:
1️⃣ Switched to Data Engineering – 1st job change (2016)
 2️⃣ Built our first family home in my hometown (2017)
 3️⃣ Got married (2018)
 4️⃣ Purchased my first car after 5 years of patience (2019)
 5️⃣ Welcomed our first baby boy (2020)
 6️⃣ Upgraded to an iPhone after 7 years of waiting (2022)
 7️⃣ Welcomed our second baby boy (2023)
 8️⃣ 2nd job change to continue growing (2024)
 9️⃣ Achieved a 9x salary growth compared to my first paycheck
 🔟 And the one thing that didn’t change in all these years – my weight! (2025) 😅
This journey reminds me that progress comes in many forms — career, family, financial growth, and personal milestones. While some goals take time, patience and consistency always pay off.
Here’s to the next decade of growth, learning, and maybe… cracking that weight challenge too! 💪✨
